title: Cash and Debt Forecasting
summary: This page is a navigational/informational overview from Treasury.gov describing the three main Treasury reporting mechanisms: the Daily Treasury Statement (DTS), the Monthly Treasury Statement of Receipts and Outlays (MTS), and the Monthly Statement of the Public Debt (MSPD). It provides no current data, forecasts, or market-moving information. This is a procedural reference page, not an archiveable research document or data release.
key_points: The Daily Treasury Statement (DTS) summarizes Treasury cash and debt operations and is available by 4:00 p.m. the following business day
Treasury's operating cash is maintained at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York and in Tax and Loan accounts at commercial banks
The Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) is released on the 8th workday of the month following the reporting month
The Monthly Statement of the Public Debt (MSPD) is published on the fourth business day of each month and includes five tables on outstanding Treasury securities and debt limit status
why_it_matters: This page serves as a reference guide for locating Treasury fiscal data but contains no specific forecasts, current cash position data, or debt figures. It does not provide actionable macro catalyst information and has no archival value as standalone content.
